metadata.name: ContentBasedRouter
metadata.description: Route incoming orders based on priority and total: HIGH priority or total > $10,000 goes to high-priority processing; all others to standard processing.

biztalkInput.orchestrationXml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-16"?>
<!--
  BizTalk Orchestration (ODX) — Content-Based Routing via Decide Shape
  =====================================================================
  Shapes: Receive (activating) → Decide (two branches by priority/total) → Send (two output ports)
  Pattern: Content-Based Routing using orchestration-level conditional logic
  Complexity: Simple–Moderate

  MIGRATION KEY POINTS:
  - Decide shape → If (Condition) action in Logic Apps
  - XLANG/s expression (C#-like syntax) → WDL expression (JSON predicate format)
  - Two Send shapes for different destinations → two parallel branches in If action
  - FILE adapter → Blob Storage or ServiceBus depending on target architecture
-->
<om:Module xmlns:om="http://schemas.microsoft.com/BizTalk/2003/DesignerData">
  <om:Property Name="InitialCodeFile" Value="OrderRouter.odx"/>
  <om:Property Name="InitialNamespace" Value="ContentBasedRouting"/>
  <om:Property Name="InitialTypeName" Value="OrderRouterOrchestration"/>
  <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="Routes incoming orders to high-priority or standard processing based on Priority field and OrderTotal"/>

  <om:Service Name="OrderRouterOrchestration">
    <om:Property Name="Type" Value="Public"/>
    <om:Property Name="Compensation" Value="Default"/>
    <om:Property Name="Transaction" Value="None"/>

    <!--
      Message declarations.
      Note: all three messages use the same schema type.
      IncomingOrder is the original; HighPriorityOrder and StandardOrder are
      used for the two routing branches (message assignment copies IncomingOrder).
    -->
    <om:MessageDeclaration Name="IncomingOrder" Type="ContentBasedRouting.Schemas.OrderMessage"/>
    <om:MessageDeclaration Name="HighPriorityOrder" Type="ContentBasedRouting.Schemas.OrderMessage"/>
    <om:MessageDeclaration Name="StandardOrder" Type="ContentBasedRouting.Schemas.OrderMessage"/>

    <!--
      Port declarations — three ports: one inbound, two outbound (one per routing branch).
      Migration: Receive port → Trigger; Send ports → two possible Action paths in If branches.
    -->
    <om:PortDeclaration Name="ReceiveOrderPort"
                        Type="ContentBasedRouting.PortTypes.IReceiveOrder"
                        Modifier="Private"
                        Polarity="Implements"/>

    <om:PortDeclaration Name="HighPrioritySendPort"
                        Type="ContentBasedRouting.PortTypes.ISendHighPriorityOrder"
                        Modifier="Private"
                        Polarity="Uses"/>

    <om:PortDeclaration Name="StandardSendPort"
                        Type="ContentBasedRouting.PortTypes.ISendStandardOrder"
                        Modifier="Private"
                        Polarity="Uses"/>

    <om:Body>
      <om:Shape Name="BeginOrchestration" Type="BodyShape">

        <!-- RECEIVE — creates orchestration instance when order file arrives (FILE adapter) -->
        <om:Shape Name="ReceiveOrder" Type="ReceiveShape">
          <om:Property Name="ActivatesCorrelation" Value="true"/>
          <om:Property Name="Message" Value="IncomingOrder"/>
          <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="Activating receive — one instance per incoming order file"/>
          <om:Port Name="ReceiveOrderPort"/>
        </om:Shape>

        <!--
          DECIDE SHAPE — the CBR decision point.
          DecideBranch shapes represent the IF/ELSE branches.
          The first non-else branch whose Expression evaluates to true is taken.
          Only the LAST branch may use "else" as its expression.

          XLANG/s expression syntax:
          - Uses C#-like operators: ==, !=, &&, ||, >, <, >=, <=
          - Message field access: MessageName.FieldName (promoted property XPath shorthand)
          - String literals: quoted with ""
          - Numeric literals: unquoted (10000.0, not "10000.0")

          Migration → Logic Apps:
          - Decide with 2 branches → If action with true/false paths
          - Decide with 3+ branches → nested If actions or Switch action
          - XLANG/s  a || b  → WDL  "or": [{condition1}, {condition2}]
          - XLANG/s  a && b  → WDL  "and": [{condition1}, {condition2}]
        -->
        <om:Shape Name="RouteByPriority" Type="DecideShape">
          <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="Route order to high-priority or standard processing path"/>

          <!--
            Branch 1: HIGH PRIORITY
            Condition: Priority is "HIGH" OR total exceeds $10,000
            XLANG/s: IncomingOrder.Priority == "HIGH" || IncomingOrder.OrderTotal > 10000.0
            WDL: { "or": [ {"equals": [..., "HIGH"]}, {"greater": [..., 10000]} ] }
          -->
          <om:Shape Name="HighPriorityBranch" Type="DecideBranch">
            <om:Property Name="Expression">
              <![CDATA[IncomingOrder.Priority == "HIGH" || IncomingOrder.OrderTotal > 10000.0]]>
            </om:Property>
            <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="High priority: Priority=HIGH or OrderTotal > $10,000"/>

            <!--
              Construct the HighPriorityOrder message by copying IncomingOrder.
              MessageAssignment is the simplest BizTalk message construction:
              just assigns one message to another (no transformation).
              Migration: this becomes a Compose action or is eliminated if we send directly.
            -->
            <om:Shape Name="ConstructHighPriority" Type="ConstructShape">
              <om:Property Name="Messages" Value="HighPriorityOrder"/>
              <om:Shape Name="AssignHighPriority" Type="MessageAssignmentShape">
                <om:Property Name="Expression">
                  <![CDATA[HighPriorityOrder = IncomingOrder;]]>
                </om:Property>
              </om:Shape>
            </om:Shape>

            <!-- Send the high-priority order to the high-priority send port -->
            <om:Shape Name="SendHighPriority" Type="SendShape">
              <om:Property Name="Message" Value="HighPriorityOrder"/>
              <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="Send to HighPriority FILE output path"/>
              <om:Port Name="HighPrioritySendPort"/>
            </om:Shape>
          </om:Shape>

          <!--
            Branch 2: STANDARD (else — all other orders)
            The "else" expression is the default branch (no condition needed).
            Migration: becomes the "else" path of the If action.
          -->
          <om:Shape Name="StandardBranch" Type="DecideBranch">
            <om:Property Name="Expression" Value="else"/>
            <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="Standard priority: all orders not matching high-priority criteria"/>

            <om:Shape Name="ConstructStandard" Type="ConstructShape">
              <om:Property Name="Messages" Value="StandardOrder"/>
              <om:Shape Name="AssignStandard" Type="MessageAssignmentShape">
                <om:Property Name="Expression">
                  <![CDATA[StandardOrder = IncomingOrder;]]>
                </om:Property>
              </om:Shape>
            </om:Shape>

            <om:Shape Name="SendStandard" Type="SendShape">
              <om:Property Name="Message" Value="StandardOrder"/>
              <om:Property Name="Notes" Value="Send to Standard FILE output path"/>
              <om:Port Name="StandardSendPort"/>
            </om:Shape>
          </om:Shape>

        </om:Shape>
        <!-- End Decide shape -->

      </om:Shape>
    </om:Body>
  </om:Service>
</om:Module>
biztalkInput.bindingXml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<!--
  BizTalk Binding File — Content-Based Routing
  =============================================
  Two send ports with FILTER CONDITIONS demonstrate how BizTalk does subscription-based CBR.
  Even in orchestration-based CBR (like this fixture), the bindings show the send port
  filter syntax — useful for the migration engine to understand both CBR approaches.

  MIGRATION NOTE — Two CBR Approaches:

  Approach A (this fixture): ORCHESTRATION-based CBR
    Orchestration has Decide shape → sends to two different bound send ports.
    Logic Apps: single workflow with If action, two branches writing to two destinations.

  Approach B: MESSAGEBOX subscription-based CBR (no orchestration)
    Send port filters create subscriptions in MessageBox.
    When a message arrives, BizTalk evaluates all subscriptions and routes automatically.
    Logic Apps equivalent: separate workflows with different trigger conditions,
    OR a router workflow that evaluates conditions and calls downstream workflows.
-->
<BindingInfo xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
  <Description>Content-Based Routing Bindings — TEST ENVIRONMENT</Description>
  <Timestamp>2026-02-23T00:00:00</Timestamp>
  <Version>3.5.1.0</Version>

  <ModuleRefCollection>
    <ModuleRef DisplayName="ContentBasedRouting(1.0.0.0)" Name="ContentBasedRouting">
      <ServiceRefCollection>
        <ServiceRef Name="OrderRouterOrchestration" FullName="ContentBasedRouting.OrderRouterOrchestration" Type="1">
          <Ports>
            <Port Name="ReceiveOrderPort" PortModifier="2">
              <ReceivePortRef Name="RP_ReceiveOrders_FILE"/>
            </Port>
            <Port Name="HighPrioritySendPort" PortModifier="1">
              <SendPortRef Name="SP_HighPriority_FILE"/>
            </Port>
            <Port Name="StandardSendPort" PortModifier="1">
              <SendPortRef Name="SP_Standard_FILE"/>
            </Port>
          </Ports>
        </ServiceRef>
      </ServiceRefCollection>
    </ModuleRef>
  </ModuleRefCollection>

  <SendPortCollection>

    <!--
      HIGH PRIORITY send port.
      Adapter: FILE → writes to C:\Output\HighPriority\
      Filter: ContentBasedRouting.Priority == "HIGH"

      MIGRATION NOTE on filters:
      When an orchestration explicitly sends to this port (via bound port), the filter is
      technically redundant — the orchestration controls routing. However, if this were
      a standalone send port (no orchestration), the filter creates the MessageBox subscription.

      Filter condition shown here is for PROMOTED PROPERTY-based routing.
      Migration → Logic Apps: promoted property filters map to:
        - Service Bus filter expressions (if messages go through Service Bus)
        - Logic Apps Condition expressions (if evaluated inline in workflow)
    -->
    <SendPort Name="SP_HighPriority_FILE"
              IsStatic="true"
              IsTwoWay="false"
              BindingOption="0">

      <PrimaryTransport>
        <Address>C:\Output\HighPriority\%SourceFileName%</Address>
        <TransportType Name="FILE" Capabilities="11" ConfigurationClsid="5e49e3a6-b4fc-4077-b44c-22f34a242fdb"/>
        <TransportTypeData>
          <![CDATA[<CustomProps><CopyMode vt="19">0</CopyMode><AllowCacheOnWrite vt="11">0</AllowCacheOnWrite></CustomProps>]]>
        </TransportTypeData>
        <RetryCount>3</RetryCount>
        <RetryInterval>5</RetryInterval>
      </PrimaryTransport>

      <SendPipelineName>Microsoft.BizTalk.DefaultPipelines.PassThruTransmit, Microsoft.BizTalk.DefaultPipelines, Version=3.0.1.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=31bf3856ad364e35</SendPipelineName>
      <Description>High-priority order output — high-value or flagged orders</Description>

      <!--
        FILTER — subscription-based CBR condition (for reference).
        Operator codes: 0 = equals, 1 = contains, 2 = starts with
        These promoted property filters are what BizTalk evaluates against the MessageBox.
        In orchestration-mode (this fixture), the orchestration controls routing instead.
      -->
      <Filter>
        <Group>
          <!-- Message type subscription -->
          <Statement Property="BTS.MessageType"
                     Operator="0"
                     Value="http://ContentBasedRouting.Schemas.OrderMessage#Order"/>
          <!-- Promoted property filter: Priority = "HIGH" -->
          <Statement Property="ContentBasedRouting.Priority"
                     Operator="0"
                     Value="HIGH"/>
        </Group>
        <!-- OR: high-value order (OrderTotal > 10000 — not directly expressible in filter syntax)
             Note: BizTalk filter syntax does NOT support > / < operators on numeric promoted properties.
             This is a known BizTalk CBR limitation — complex numeric routing requires orchestration. -->
      </Filter>

    </SendPort>

    <!-- STANDARD send port — all orders not matching high-priority criteria -->
    <SendPort Name="SP_Standard_FILE"
              IsStatic="true"
              IsTwoWay="false"
              BindingOption="0">

      <PrimaryTransport>
        <Address>C:\Output\Standard\%SourceFileName%</Address>
        <TransportType Name="FILE" Capabilities="11" ConfigurationClsid="5e49e3a6-b4fc-4077-b44c-22f34a242fdb"/>
        <TransportTypeData>
          <![CDATA[<CustomProps><CopyMode vt="19">0</CopyMode><AllowCacheOnWrite vt="11">0</AllowCacheOnWrite></CustomProps>]]>
        </TransportTypeData>
        <RetryCount>3</RetryCount>
        <RetryInterval>5</RetryInterval>
      </PrimaryTransport>

      <SendPipelineName>Microsoft.BizTalk.DefaultPipelines.PassThruTransmit, Microsoft.BizTalk.DefaultPipelines, Version=3.0.1.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=31bf3856ad364e35</SendPipelineName>
      <Description>Standard priority order output — normal orders</Description>

      <Filter>
        <Group>
          <Statement Property="BTS.MessageType"
                     Operator="0"
                     Value="http://ContentBasedRouting.Schemas.OrderMessage#Order"/>
          <Statement Property="ContentBasedRouting.Priority"
                     Operator="0"
                     Value="NORMAL"/>
        </Group>
      </Filter>

    </SendPort>

  </SendPortCollection>

  <ReceivePortCollection>
    <ReceivePort Name="RP_ReceiveOrders_FILE" IsTwoWay="false">
      <Description>Receives order files for CBR routing</Description>
      <ReceiveLocationCollection>
        <ReceiveLocation Name="RL_ReceiveOrders_FILE">
          <Address>C:\Input\Orders\*.xml</Address>
          <TransportType Name="FILE" Capabilities="11" ConfigurationClsid="5e49e3a6-b4fc-4077-b44c-22f34a242fdb"/>
          <TransportTypeData>
            <![CDATA[<CustomProps><PollingInterval vt="3">30000</PollingInterval><FileMask vt="8">*.xml</FileMask><BatchSize vt="3">20</BatchSize></CustomProps>]]>
          </TransportTypeData>
          <ReceivePipelineName>Microsoft.BizTalk.DefaultPipelines.XMLReceive, Microsoft.BizTalk.DefaultPipelines, Version=3.0.1.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=31bf3856ad364e35</ReceivePipelineName>
          <Description>Polls C:\Input\Orders for *.xml files every 30 seconds</Description>
          <Enable>true</Enable>
        </ReceiveLocation>
      </ReceiveLocationCollection>
    </ReceivePort>
  </ReceivePortCollection>

</BindingInfo>

trigger.source: Azure Blob Storage (orders-inbound container, polling every 1 minute)

steps.description: Route order by priority: HIGH priority or OrderTotal > $10,000 goes to high-priority processing

trueBranch.description: Write high-priority order to high-priority blob container

falseBranch.description: Write standard order to standard processing blob container
